mission.title: gitea: Integrate or Interface Docker Registry
mission.description: Integrate or Interface Docker Registry. Requested by 118+ users.

steps.title: Check current gitea setup
steps.description:
Verify your gitea version and configuration:
```bash
gitea --version
```
This feature requires a working gitea installation.

steps.title: Review gitea configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant gitea configuration:
I know, this may be completely out of scope for this project. But I still try:

It would be cool if gitea could provide support for a docker registry.

Full integration is probably very unlikely to ever being added.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Integrate or Interface Docker Registry
steps.description:
For third party authentication, they have various files in that authn directory that connect to various authentication providers. So there are various options, 1. complete #27 to add an Oauth provider to Gitea and then you have the GitHub provider in cesanta that you can re-use (this option
```yaml
{
  "admin": true,
  "pull": true,
  "push": true
}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Integrate or Interface Docker Registry" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: For third party authentication, they have various files in that authn directory that connect to various authentication providers. So there are various options, 1. complete #27 to add an Oauth provider to Gitea and then you have the GitHub provider in cesanta that you can re-use (this option requires work for both projects). Option 2.

prerequisites.description: A working gitea installation or development environment.
